mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, reducing borrowing costs and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth indicates robust labor market conditions, increasing consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of payroll growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening demand for residential construction, reducing corporate revenues and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 1100 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signal monetary tightening pressures, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing borrowing costs and reducing corporate profits, which drive negative S&P 500 returns during inflationary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances during periods when CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances when INDPRO rises by >1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment (UNRATE > 6.0%) signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending and corporate revenues, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE > 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals economic overheating, increasing policy uncertainty and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where both GDPC1 > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.
